prompt: Vertical and applied AI startups (legal, healthcare, finance, sales, support, marketing, security, insurance, construction, etc.) that raised $50M+ in 2025-2026. For each: name, vertical, amount, date, wedge. Cite sources.
content:
**Several vertical/applied AI startups in sectors like healthcare, legal, and customer support raised $50M+ (often much more) in 2025–2026.** Here are prominent examples based on available reports (note that funding data can vary slightly by source due to extensions, announcements, or valuations including/excluding new capital; “wedge” refers to the company’s core specialized focus/product within its vertical).[[1]](https://www.abridge.com/blog/series-e)[[2]](https://www.harvey.ai/blog/harvey-raises-at-dollar11-billion-valuation-to-scale-agents-across-law-firms-and-enterprises)[[3]](https://decagon.ai/blog/series-d-announcement)[[4]](https://www.parloa.com/parloa-in-the-press/parloa-valued-at-3-billion-with-350m-series-d/)

- **Abridge** (Healthcare): $300M Series E (June 2025; also a prior $250M Series D in Feb 2025), valuation ~$5.3B. Wedge: Generative AI platform for ambient clinical conversation transcription, documentation, revenue cycle intelligence, and care workflows at the point of care (deployed across 100+ health systems).[[1]](https://www.abridge.com/blog/series-e)[[5]](https://www.fiercehealthcare.com/ai-and-machine-learning/ambient-ai-startup-abridge-scores-300m-series-e-backed-a16z-and-khosla)

- **Harvey** (Legal): Multiple large rounds including $300M Series D (Feb 2025 at ~$3B valuation), $300M Series E (June 2025 at ~$5B), $160M (Dec 2025 at ~$8B), and $200M growth round (March 2026 at $11B valuation). Total funding exceeded $1B. Wedge: AI legal infrastructure/platform with custom agents for end-to-end legal work, research, drafting, and workflows tailored for law firms and in-house teams.[[2]](https://www.harvey.ai/blog/harvey-raises-at-dollar11-billion-valuation-to-scale-agents-across-law-firms-and-enterprises)[[6]](https://www.harvey.ai/blog/harvey-raises-series-e)

- **Decagon** (Customer support/sales): $250M Series D (January 2026), valuation tripled to $4.5B. Wedge: Enterprise conversational AI platform and proactive AI agents/concierge for automated, multi-channel customer support (chat, email, etc.) serving global enterprises.[[3]](https://decagon.ai/blog/series-d-announcement)[[7]](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-01-28/ai-customer-support-startup-decagon-valued-at-4-5-billion)

- **Parloa** (Customer experience/support): $350M Series D (January 2026; closed ~7–8 months after prior round), valuation $3B. Total raised >$560M. Wedge: Agentic AI platform for enterprise customer experience, focusing on AI agents to elevate CX across contact centers and digital channels.[[4]](https://www.parloa.com/parloa-in-the-press/parloa-valued-at-3-billion-with-350m-series-d/)[[8]](https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/15/parloa-triples-its-valuation-in-8-months-to-3b-with-350m-raise/)

**Additional notes on near-matches or smaller rounds**:  
- Applied AI (regulated industries including healthcare/insurance/banking): ~$55M Series A (Feb 2025) for its Opus AI-native workflow/automation platform.[[9]](https://www.fwdstart.me/p/applied-ai-raises-pre-series-b-from-mubadala-and-arbor-ventures-to-scale-enterprise-workflow-platfor)  
- Midi Health (women’s/midlife health): $50M Series C (2025) with AI-powered search and care platform.[[10]](https://dot.la/california-ai-law-eazewell-westbrook-2674152032.html)  

These stand out from broader vertical AI funding trends in 2025 (where verticals captured a significant share of deals, with mega-rounds in healthcare and legal among the largest). Data is drawn from company announcements, PitchBook/Tracxn-style trackers, and news reports; exact figures can include extensions or vary by reporting. Additional startups likely exist but were not as prominently detailed in results. For the most current or exhaustive lists, specialized databases like PitchBook or Crunchbase are recommended.
